contacts.name: Grace Dickow
contacts.title: Principal & Founder
contacts.dept: Grace Dickow's Passion Is To Introduce Students To A Deep, Close Relationship With Jesus Christ. As An Educator And Founder Of Valeo Academy, Grace Empowers Teachers And Students To Experience Their God-given Potential. Receiving Her Teaching Degree From The University Of Illinois, Her Down-to-earth Communication Style Quickly Removes Barriers, Making It Easy For People Of All Ages To Receive God's Unconditional Love And Limitless Power For Their Lives.

contacts.name: Ashley Hines
contacts.title: Vice Principal Of School Administration
contacts.dept: Ashley Hines, A Passionate And Dedicated Educator, Brings Over Thirteen Years Of Teaching Experience In History, Civics, And Economics At The Secondary Level. In 2023, She Made A Seamless Transition To The Administrative Team At Valeo Academy. Ashley Holds A Bachelor’s Degree In History Education From Old Dominion University And A Master’s In School Administration From Grand Canyon University. She Is Committed To Fostering A Thriving Academic Environment While Guiding Students To Cultivate A Deep And Lasting Connection With God. Her Unwavering Dedication To Character Development And Building Lifelong Habits Of Success Makes Her An Invaluable Part Of The Valeo Academy Community.

contacts.name: Dunia Baca
contacts.title: Vice Principal Of Operations
contacts.dept: Dunia Baca Serves As Vice Principal Of Operations At Valeo Academy, Where She Brings Strong Leadership, Administrative Excellence, And A Heart For Christ-centered Education. In Addition To Her Role In School Leadership, She Has Taught A Range Of Subjects Including Spanish, Health, Economics, Consumer Education, Mathematics, And Fine Arts. All Four Of Her Children Are Proud Valeo Academy Graduates. She And Her Husband, Galo, Also Serve On The Pastoral Support Team At Life Changers Church.

contacts.name: You Belong Here
contacts.title: Lab Admissions
contacts.dept: The University Of Chicago Laboratory Schools Are Home To The Youngest Members Of The University Of Chicago's Academic Community. We Ignite And Nurture An Enduring Spirit Of Scholarship, Curiosity, Creativity, And Confidence. We Value Learning Experientially, Exhibiting Kindness, And Honoring Diversity.

contacts.name: Roni Facen
contacts.title: the school appointed Dr. Roni Facen, an alumna, educator, and community activist, as Principal and CEO. Dr. Facen credits her St. Francis de Sales education for providing her with opportunities. The belief and support from teachers and staff fueled her potential, and she aims to offer similar opportunities to current students. Recognizing the significance of St. Francis de Sales High School to the South East Side of Chicago and the new generation of Pioneers, she is dedicated to its importance to the community.

contacts.name: Eve Wade
contacts.title: Dean of Faculty and Staff Formation

contacts.name: Dave Schlagel
contacts.title: Pastor Schlagel came to Marquette Manor in January of 2010. Before coming to Marquette, he served as an associate pastor in Oregon for six years. Prior to that, he served as a Christian school administrator and as a Bible college dean in Wisconsin.

contacts.name: Under the
contacts.title: American with Disabilities Act of 1991, this program is required to reasonably accommodate individuals with a disability. The reasonable accommodation requirement applies to the application process, any pre-employment testing, interviews and actual employment, but ONLY if the program supervisor is made aware that an accommodation is required. If you are disabled and require accommodation, you may request it at ANY time during the interview process. You are obligated to inform the Principal of your needs if it will impact your ability to perform the job for which you are applying. "I understand that I can request an accommodation can be requested during the interview process." (please answer yes or no)

contacts.name: 2026/2027 admissions
contacts.title: HOPE CHRISTIAN ACADEMY admits students of any race, color, national, and ethnic origin to all the rights, privileges, programs, and activities generally accorded or made available to students in the academy. It does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, gender, national, and ethnic origin in the administration of its educational policies, admissions policies, scholarship and loan programs, and athletic and other school-administered programs.
